news.headline: Standard Chartered Hong Kong: HKD Best Lending Rate Unchanged
Trader warns against heavy leverage on FOMC meeting, $188.77M liquidated, Bitcoin up 3.49%, BTC at $87,427 briefly. Source: CoinMarketCap.
news.summary: Following the Federal Reserve's decision to keep the US interest rates unchanged, the crypto market experienced an upswing, catching traders who had bet on a downside off guard. MN Trading Capital founder Michael van de Poppe warned against heavy leverage usage, emphasizing the risks. CoinGlass data indicated $188.77 million liquidated from the market, with $127.80 million from short positions. Bitcoin surged 3.84% to $87,427 post-Powell's speech before declining to $85,760. Total liquidated short positions amounted to approximately $257.03 million over the past 24 hours. Ether rose by 2.27%, XRP by 2.40%, and Bitcoin by 3.49% in the same period. Van de Poppe highlighted the significance of Powell's words on defining Bitcoin's future price actions. However, analysts like Crypto trading account BitcoinHyper and Matt Mena forecasted a potential short-term boost in Bitcoin due to the Federal Reserve's "dovish shift" but caution against new long positions at current levels. Powell projected interest rates at 3.9% by 2025 and 3.4% by 2026. The broader macro environment supports a bullish outlook for BTC despite near-term uncertainties. The article concluded by advising readers to conduct their research on investment decisions.

news.headline: Jabil to release Q2 results with earnings expected at $1.83/share on $6.4B revenue. Shares rose 2.9% to $139.48.
news.summary: Tech giant Jabil Inc. (NYSE:JBL) is set to unveil its second-quarter financial results on March 20. Analysts predict earnings of $1.83 per share for the quarter, up from $1.68 per share a year ago. The company anticipates quarterly revenue of $6.4 billion, a drop from $6.77 billion in the prior year. In collaboration with Apptronik, Jabil announced plans to develop Apollo humanoid robots for use in specific manufacturing processes. Jabil's stock price closed at $139.48, marking a 2.9% increase on Wednesday. For more detailed information, readers can access the full story on Benzinga.com, including the latest analyst ratings and other insights.

news.headline: Zeekr Group's Q4 2024: 79,250 vehicle deliveries, 39.2% revenue increase, 19.0% gross margin, 51.3% decrease in loss from operations. FY2024: 222,123 total deliveries, 46.9% revenue increase. CEO plans further expansion.
news.summary: ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited reported unaudited financial results for Q4 and full year 2024. In Q4, total vehicle deliveries were 79,250 units (99.8% YoY increase) and vehicle sales were RMB19,301.6 million. Total revenues were RMB22,777.8 million, with gross profit of RMB4,316.5 million. Net loss was RMB820.6 million. For full year 2024, total revenues were RMB75,912.7 million, with RMB55,315.3 million from vehicle sales. Gross profit for the year was RMB12,447.5 million. Cash and cash equivalents were RMB8,961.7 million as of December 31, 2024. The company announced strategic developments, new product launches, and CEO and CFO comments regarding future strategies in expanding their product lineup and global presence.

news.summary: Discover how over the course of the last 20 years, NVIDIA Research has played a crucial role in advancing AI, graphics, and computing technologies, leading to substantial breakthroughs. This has significantly impacted key product advancements in the industry.

news.headline: Balatro wins Game of the Year at 25th Game Developers Choice Awards, with 4 total wins; premiere awards in digital games industry.
news.summary: In San Francisco, the indie roguelike deckbuilding game Balatro has emerged victorious at the 25th annual Game Developers Choice Awards (GDCA). The game clinched the prestigious Game of the Year Award along with wins in the categories of Best Debut, Innovation Award, and Best Design, accumulating a remarkable four wins overall. The GDCAs stand as the foremost honors for peer acknowledgment in the digital games sector, acknowledging and lauding the creativity and excellence within the industry.

Cryptocurrency aids in fentanyl trade, with blockchain crucial in combatting issue globally, per Chainalysis.
news.summary: The use of cryptocurrency in fentanyl trafficking is on the rise, as reported by Chainalysis. Their analysis suggests that blockchain technology is providing crucial means to address this pressing global concern.

Brigadier General Paul Craft (Ret.) named President-Defense at DataShapes AI. Longest-serving Chief of Cyber for U.S. Army.
news.summary: The former Chief of Cyber for the U.S. Army, who held the position for the longest tenure on record, has now taken on the role of president for DataShapes AI's defense business. This announcement was made in Charlottesville, Virginia on March 20, 2025. DataShapes AI, Inc., known for its advancements in artificial intelligence technologies, including distributed AI agents, has welcomed the experienced individual to lead their defense sector.

Partnership between BenQ Medical Technology and Brain Navi to advance neurosurgical robotics in China, enhancing precision and innovation.
news.summary: Brain Navi Biotechnology, a prominent player in neurosurgical robotics, has revealed a key collaboration with BenQ Medical Technology in China. The partnership aims to boost precision and innovation in the field. The announcement came on March 19, 2025, in Zhubei. The collaboration is set to introduce new technologies and bring them to market to enhance neurosurgical procedures. This move is expected to have a significant impact on the industry in China.

news.headline: NVIDIA boosts AI on virtual platforms with vGPU 18.0, backing Windows Server 2025 and Proxmox VE, and offering new AI toolkits.
news.summary: The recent release of NVIDIA's vGPU 18.0 boosts AI potential on virtual platforms, with added support for Microsoft Windows Server 2025 and Proxmox VE. Alongside, it unveils fresh AI toolkits aimed at developers to improve functionality.

news.headline: Meme coins rally as Bonk leads with 8% increase in 24 hours, 140% rise in trading volume, and 'Buy' signal from indicator.
news.summary: Alternative coins experienced a notable increase on Wednesday amidst a general upsurge in the cryptocurrency market. The primary story was the rise of Bonk, a crypto based on Solana (CRYPTO: SOL), which saw an impressive 8% surge in the past day. This dog-themed coin also saw its trading volume spike by 140%, indicating a substantial increase in buying activity. Trading indicators pointed towards a positive trend for Bonk, with the Moving Average Convergence Divergence signaling a "Buy" status as of the latest data. Despite this, the Relative Strength Index was still in a neutral territory. For further details, refer to the full news story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Two CUPE locals end strikes, 92% of CUPE 40 at Calgary Board of Education and 87% of CUPE 3484 at Black Gold accept settlements.
news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta, two additional CUPE locals have approved tentative agreements, effectively bringing an end to strikes at the Calgary Board of Education and the Black Gold School Division. Members of CUPE 40 at the Calgary Board of Education voted overwhelmingly, with 92% in favor of the settlement, while members of CUPE 3484 at the Black Gold School Division voted 87% in favor. Employees are set to return to work at the Black Gold School Division tomorrow, and at the Calgary Board of Education on Friday. The ratified agreements now cover the Calgary Board of Education (CUPE 40) and the Black Gold School Division.

news.headline: Elon Musk's Grok AI surprises X users, calling him top source of misinformation and hinting Trump may be a Russian asset.
news.summary: X has developed a chatbot named Grok, which is designed to prioritize truthfulness. The chatbot is informing users that Elon Musk is identified as the primary purveyor of disinformation globally. Additionally, it is hinting to users that Trump could potentially have connections to Russia as an asset.

news.headline: NVIDIA launches Blackwell Ultra, boosting AI reasoning with improved training and testing performance.
news.summary: NVIDIA has recently unveiled the Blackwell Ultra, a cutting-edge platform tailored for the AI reasoning age. This new platform boasts improved performance in training, post-training, and test-time scaling. It is designed to meet the demands of the rapidly evolving AI landscape.

Kraken closing in on $1.5B NinjaTrader deal for US crypto futures. Kraken had $1.5B revenue and $665B trading volume in 2024.
news.summary: Kraken is reportedly finalizing a $1.5 billion acquisition of NinjaTrader to expand its customer base and offer crypto futures in the US. The deal is expected to be confirmed on March 20th. With NinjaTrader as a Futures Commission Merchant, Kraken seeks to delve into equities trading and expand globally, while NinjaTrader aims for UK, Europe, and Australia markets. Kraken had $1.5 billion revenue in 2024 with 2.5 million customer accounts, while NinjaTrader serves 1.8 million customers. The acquisition follows Kraken's NFT marketplace closure to broaden its services, and it comes after the SEC dropped a lawsuit against Kraken for unregistered activities. Founded in 2011, Kraken aims to benefit from US's relaxed crypto regulations under Trump. Kraken, a top crypto exchange by spot trading volume, handles between $390 million to $4.4 billion in daily trades.

HiRO and Hallym University Sacred Heart Hospital sign MOU in Seoul, South Korea for drug development.
news.summary: In Seoul, South Korea on March 19, 2025, Harvest Integrated Research Organization (HiRO), a niche global CRO, has officially signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) with Hallym University Sacred Heart Hospital. This partnership marks a significant collaboration that aims to enhance research and development initiatives. HiRO's specialization in research will complement the hospital's expertise in medical services. The agreement signifies a mutual commitment to advancing healthcare solutions.

news.headline: Crypto exchange Garantex reportedly shifted millions to new exchange Grinex, linked by onchain evidence. Funds linked to laundering. Source: Global Ledger. Incoming transactions on Grinex almost $30M as volume hits $68M in March. Garantex under sanctions for money laundering.US collaborated with Germany, Finland to freeze domains. Tether froze $27M. Garantex halted operations, arrested Aleksej Bešciokov on money laundering charges. Magazine explores worldwide crypto laws in 2025.
news.summary: Shuttered crypto exchange Garantex reportedly reappeared under the new name Grinex after laundering over $60 million in ruble-backed stablecoins. Global Ledger revealed the connection between the two exchanges, with evidence of fund transfers. Garantex, sanctioned by the US Department of the Treasury in April 2022, was linked to criminal proceeds worth $96 billion. Tether froze $27 million tied to Garantex, resulting in a halt of operations. Aleksej Bešciokov, allegedly behind Garantex, was arrested for money laundering conspiracy. Grinex saw nearly $30 million in transactions by March 14, with a total monthly trade volume of $68 million. The exchange operates as Garantex’s successor, continuing its financial dealings.

news.headline: New sequencing models redistribute MEV in blockchain, enhancing market fairness and fostering innovation in decentralized apps. Learn more.
news.summary: Discover how the process of application-specific transaction sequencing is transforming blockchain operations by distributing Miner Extractable Value (MEV) more equitably, promoting market integrity, and encouraging creativity in decentralized applications. Learn more about this groundbreaking development.

news.headline: "Consume Me wins Seumas McNally Grand Prize & Nuovo Award at 27th Annual IGF Awards, Game Developers Conference 2025."
news.summary: In San Francisco, the indie game Consume Me, created by Jenny Jiao Hsia, AP Thomson, Jie En Lee, Violet W-P, and Ken "coda" Snyder, emerged victorious at the 27th annual Independent Games Festival Awards. This prestigious event, held as part of the 2025 Game Developers Conference, celebrates the outstanding achievements in independent gaming. Consume Me was awarded the Seumas McNally Grand Prize for Best Independent Game. This recognition highlights the game’s innovation and excellence in the past year.

news.headline: Xi highlights sustainable growth of tourism industry: Xinhua
Solana's (SOL) price surges by 7%, trading volume rises to $3.65 billion, outpacing Bitcoin and Ethereum as it gears up for the launch of first-ever SOL ETFs.
news.summary: Solana (CRYPTO: SOL) experienced a significant surge on Wednesday in anticipation of the launch of its inaugural futures exchange-traded fund (ETF) products. The cryptocurrency saw a remarkable increase of over 7%, making it the second-best performing large-cap cryptocurrency within the last 24 hours. Its trading volume also spiked by 54% to reach $3.65 billion. Despite stiff competition from big players like Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) and Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH) which grew by 3.82% and 5.11% respectively, Solana managed to outshine them all. Investors are particularly excited about the upcoming ETFs which are set to track Solana futures. This rally showcases the growing popularity and potential of Solana in the cryptocurrency market, with analysts closely monitoring its future movements.

news.headline: President Trump to speak at Digital Asset Summit in NYC on March 20, marking first time a sitting president has addressed a crypto conference.
news.summary: President Donald Trump is set to speak at the Digital Asset Summit in New York City on March 20th, marking the first time a sitting president will address a crypto industry conference. The event follows a speech by Bo Hines, Executive Director of the White House's Presidents Council of Advisers on Digital Assets, who hinted at the administration's interest in accumulating bitcoin. Trump's address is anticipated to bring attention to the summit, which has seen significant growth, with a notable increase in attendance this year. This development underscores the growing influence of digital assets within the political landscape.

news.headline: Bakkt appoints new co-CEO, focuses on crypto services post client loss. Partnered with DTR, sells trust to parent co. for $1.5M. Revenues up 350% to $3.49B in 2024, net loss down to $103.4M. Stock down 62.5% in 2025.
news.summary: Crypto firm Bakkt Holdings has appointed a new co-CEO, Akshay Naheta, in a bid to focus on its core crypto offerings after losing major clients like Bank of America and Webull. The company is cutting some services and selling its crypto custody subsidiary to parent company Intercontinental Exchange for $1.5 million, aiming to save $3.8 million in operating costs annually. Despite a recent share price tumble, Bakkt reported a revenue of $3.49 billion in 2024, a 350% increase year-over-year, with a narrowed net loss of $103.4 million. Forecasting a revenue increase of nearly 50% for the first quarter of 2025, Bakkt shares closed flat at $9.31 on March 19, down 62.5% for the year and essentially erasing all gains since October 2021. The company is looking to enhance its stablecoin payments and crypto trading capabilities by integrating DTR's infrastructure. This move illustrates Bakkt's strategic shift towards prioritizing its crypto-related services and offerings.

news.headline: JOYY sees steady profit growth, with 2024 revenue hitting US$2.24B. US$309.2M in buybacks, quarterly dividends of approx US$50M.
news.summary: JOYY Inc. reported its unaudited financial results for Q4 and full year 2024, with revenue reaching US$549.4 million in Q4, BIGO generating US$480.0 million. The full-year revenue was US$2.24 billion, BIGO's revenue being US$1.99 billion, a 3.3% YoY increase. The non-GAAP net profit for 2024 was US$298.5 million, up 2.0% YoY with a margin of 13.3%. The operating profit was US$136.1 million, a 4.2% YoY increase with a margin of 6.1%. JOYY announced a quarterly dividend policy of US$50 million per quarter and a share repurchase program of up to US$300 million by Dec 2027. They also changed their Nasdaq ticker symbol to JOYY from YY, effective March 31, 2025. CEO Ms. Ting Li highlighted operational efficiency improvements and revenue diversification, emphasizing commitment to growth and shareholder returns. Livestreaming revenues grew by 55.9% to US$449.8 million YoY.
